docs.title: BigCommerce Page Builder Widgets - PapaThemes Docs
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for PapaThemes BigCommerce Widgets.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PapaThemes Widgets is a collection of custom widgets designed for BigCommerce Page Builder. These widgets help you easily add rich UI components to your website without writing code.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• AI Widgets

    AI-powered content creation widgets

    AI HTML Widget

  • Basic Widgets

    Core widgets for common UI patterns

    Accordion Image Gallery CKEditor Responsive

  • Beautify Widgets

    Enhanced widgets for Beautify themes

    Flex Banners Products Feed Category Icons

  • Theme-Specific

    Widgets for specific PapaThemes themes

    Chiara Theme Dinosaur Theme Eyeva Theme

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Page Builder Compatible - Works seamlessly with BigCommerce's drag-and-drop interface
  • ✅ Flexible Customization - Multiple configuration options for each widget
  • ✅ Responsive Design - Displays beautifully on all devices
  • ✅ Inline Editing - Supports direct content editing on the page
  • ✅ Multi-Theme Support - Compatible with multiple PapaThemes themes

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Install the PapaThemes app from BigCommerce App Marketplace
  2. Open Page Builder in your BigCommerce admin
  3. Find PapaThemes widgets in the widget panel
  4. Drag and drop widgets to your page
  5. Configure widgets using the settings panel

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
Need help? Contact us:

  • Website: papathemes.com
  • Email: support@papathemes.com

Documentation Version: 1.0.0 | Last Updated: January 2026

docs.title: Accordion Widget for BigCommerce Page Builder
Description
docs.text:
The Accordion Widget displays a list of expandable and collapsible items. Users can click on a heading to show or hide the content inside.

Page Builder Name: Accordion | PapaThemes

Version: 1.0.0

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Multiple accordion items in one widget
  • ✅ Customizable colors and fonts
  • ✅ HTML support in titles and content
  • ✅ Inline content editing
  • ✅ Responsive on all devices
  • ✅ Accessibility support (ARIA)

docs.title: 1. Add Widget
docs.text:
1. Open Page Builder
  2. Drag Accordion | PapaThemes widget to the page
  3. Widget displays with 2 default items

docs.title: 2. General Configuration
docs.text: In the Settings tab:

docs.title: General
docs.text: Option Description Default Border Color Accordion border color #858585 Focus Color Color when item is focused #0759d5

docs.title: Heading

docs.title: Body
docs.text: Option Description Default Color Body text color #000000 Background Color Body background #ffffff Typography Font style Default Alignment Alignment Left Padding Inner spacing 10px 15px

docs.title: 3. Managing Items
docs.text: In the Items tab:

docs.title: Adding New Items
docs.text:
1. Click Add Item button
  2. Configure content for the new item

docs.title: Configuring Each Item
docs.text: Option Description Default Open by default Expanded on page load false Title Title (HTML supported) Accordion Title Body Content (HTML supported) Accordion Body

docs.title: Deleting/Reordering Items
docs.text:
• Drag and drop to reorder
  • Click trash icon to delete

docs.title: FAQ Section
docs.text:
<!-- Title -->
<p><strong>How do I place an order?</strong></p>

<!-- Content -->
<p>You can order directly on our website by:</p>
<ol>
    <li>Select products and add to cart</li>

docs.title: Product Specifications
docs.text: Use accordion to display product specifications with sections like: - Detailed description - Technical specifications - Usage instructions - Warranty policy

docs.title: Inline Editing
docs.text:
The widget supports WYSIWYG editing:

  1. Enable Edit Mode in Page Builder
  2. Click directly on title or content
  3. Edit text on the page
  4. Changes are saved automatically

docs.title: Responsive
docs.text: Widget automatically adjusts: - Desktop: Font size per Desktop settings - Mobile (< 800px): Font size per Mobile settings

docs.title: Accessibility
docs.text: Widget follows WAI-ARIA standards: - Uses role="button" for trigger - aria-expanded attribute for state - aria-controls links trigger to panel - Supports keyboard navigation

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Performance: Avoid using more than 10 items per accordion
  2. Content: Avoid overly long content in body, may affect UX
  3. HTML: Check HTML in title/body carefully to avoid display issues

docs.title: What is the AI HTML Widget?
docs.text:
The AI HTML Widget lets you create custom HTML, CSS, and JavaScript content for your BigCommerce storefront using AI. Simply describe what you want in plain language and the built-in AI chatbot will generate production-ready code, automatically applied to your page.

Page Builder Name: AI HTML | PapaThemes

Version: 1.0.0

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ✅ AI-powered code generation via conversational chat
  • ✅ Real-time streaming responses
  • ✅ Multiple AI models (Mini, Standard, Max)
  • ✅ Code version history with navigation
  • ✅ Image paste support for visual reference
  • ✅ Page screenshot capture for style matching
  • ✅ Persistent chat history (with subscription)
  • ✅ Automatic CSS/JS scoping to prevent style conflicts
  • ✅ Responsive chatbot sidebar
  • ✅ Pre-made blocks gallery with instant preview and device frames

docs.title: How to Create Content with AI
1. Add Widget
docs.text:
1. Open Page Builder in your BigCommerce admin
  2. In the widget panel, find AI HTML | PapaThemes
  3. Drag and drop it to the desired location on your page
  4. The widget displays with default welcome content

docs.title: 2. Open the AI Chatbot
docs.text:
1. After adding the widget, a purple AI button appears at the bottom-right corner of the page
  2. Click the AI button to open the chatbot sidebar
  3. The sidebar slides in from the right with a chat interface

docs.title: 3. Generate Content
docs.text:
1. Type a description of the HTML content you want in the input box
  2. Click the Send button (or press Enter)
  3. The AI generates complete HTML/CSS/JavaScript code
  4. Code is automatically applied to your widget on the page
  5. A version entry is created so you can go back later

docs.title: 4. Refine Your Content
docs.text:
• Send follow-up messages to refine or modify the generated code
  • The AI remembers your conversation context and previous code
  • Each change creates a new version you can navigate

docs.title: Pre-made Blocks Gallery
docs.text: Instead of starting from scratch, you can browse a gallery of ready-made section templates and apply them to your widget with one click. After applying, use the AI chatbot to customize the block to match your store.

docs.title: Opening the Gallery
docs.text:
1. Open the chatbot sidebar
  2. Below the chat input, click the Browse Blocks button
  3. The gallery opens as a full-screen modal overlay

docs.title: Browsing Blocks
docs.text: The gallery displays blocks in a responsive card grid. Each card shows a desktop screenshot, the block name, and its category.

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
Filter How to Use Category Select a category from the dropdown. Each category shows the number of blocks it contains (e.g., Hero Banners (12)). Select All Categories to show everything. Tags Click the Tags button to open a multi-select dropdown. Select one or more tags to narrow results — only blocks matching all selected tags are shown. Use the search field inside the dropdown to find tags quickly. Click Clear all to reset. Search Type in the search field to filter by block name, file name, keywords, or description. Results update as you type.

All filters work together. For example, you can select a category, add tags, and type a search query at the same time.

docs.title: Infinite Scroll
docs.text: Blocks load progressively as you scroll down — 24 blocks per batch. When you reach the bottom, the next batch loads automatically. A scroll-to-top button appears in the bottom-right corner after scrolling down, letting you jump back to the top.

docs.title: Previewing a Block
docs.text: Click any card to open a lightbox preview with full-size screenshots and device frame simulation.

docs.title: Device Frames
docs.text:
Tab Description Desktop Full-width screenshot, no frame Tablet 768px width with an iPad-style frame Mobile 375px width with an iPhone-style frame

Click the tabs in the lightbox header to switch between viewports.

docs.title: Zoom Controls
docs.text: Control Action − / + Zoom out / zoom in through predefined steps (50%–300%) Reset Return to fit-to-container mode Ctrl + scroll Zoom in/out with the mouse wheel

docs.title: Applying a Block
docs.text:
1. In the lightbox preview, click Use This Block
  2. The block's HTML is fetched from the server and applied to your widget
  3. A new version is created in the version history
  4. The gallery and lightbox close automatically
  5. An inline notice appears in the chat: "Block Name applied! Tell me what to customize."

After applying a block, you can use the AI chatbot to customize it — change colors, text, layout, images, or any other aspect.

Tip

Pre-made blocks are a great starting point. Apply a block first, then tell the AI what to change — this is often faster than describing an entire design from scratch.

Browse the full template catalog

See the Section Templates Gallery for a visual catalog of all 44 form and popup templates with screenshots and descriptions.

docs.title: Header
docs.text: Element Description Status Dot Green pulsing dot indicates the chatbot is ready Model Selector Dropdown to choose AI model (Mini / Standard / Max) Version Navigation Arrow buttons to browse previous code versions (e.g., v1/3) Clear Button Trash icon to clear chat history (with confirmation) Close Button X icon to close the sidebar

docs.title: Message Area
docs.text:
• Your messages appear as purple bubbles on the right
  • AI responses appear as white bordered bubbles on the left
  • Code blocks display with syntax highlighting and a copy button
  • Thinking blocks (collapsible) show the AI's reasoning process
  • Streaming indicator (animated dots) appears while the AI generates a response

docs.title: Token Bar
docs.text:
The token bar at the bottom of the message area shows real-time usage:

Metric Icon Description Context % How much of the AI's context window is used (green/yellow/red) Input ↑ Tokens sent to the AI Output ↓ Tokens received from the AI Total Σ Total tokens used in this session Cost $ Estimated cost for this session

Click the token bar to see detailed context budget information.

docs.title: Input Area
docs.text:
• Text field: Multi-line input for describing what you want
  • Image preview: Thumbnails of pasted images appear above the input
  • Browse Blocks button: Opens the Pre-made Blocks Gallery to apply a ready-made section template
  • Send button: Purple button to submit your message

docs.title: AI Model Options and Pricing
docs.text:
Three AI models are available, each with different capabilities and pricing:

Model Best For Speed Price Mini (blue) Quick edits, simple layouts, fast iteration Fastest $24 / 1M tokens Standard (purple) Balanced quality and speed, most use cases Medium $66 / 1M tokens Max (red) Complex designs, highest quality output Slowest $108 / 1M tokens

docs.title: Choosing a Model
docs.text:
• Mini: Use for simple changes like updating text, colors, or minor layout tweaks
  • Standard: Recommended for most tasks including new sections, forms, and interactive elements
  • Max: Use for complex, multi-component layouts or when you need the highest quality output

To change the model, click the model selector dropdown in the chatbot header. Your selection is saved for future sessions.

docs.title: Version Control
docs.text: Every time the AI generates or updates code, a new version is saved automatically.

docs.title: Navigating Versions
docs.text:
• The version counter (e.g., v2/5) shows your current position
  • Click ← to go to the previous version
  • Click → to go to the next version
  • The widget content updates immediately when you navigate

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. AI generates code → Version v1 created
  2. You request a change → Version v2 created
  3. You can go back to v1 at any time
  4. If you go back to v1 and send a new message, the AI uses v1 code as the starting point

Version history requires the Storage Subscription

Without an active subscription, versions are only kept in the browser session and will be lost when you close the tab. With the Storage Subscription, all versions are saved to the server and persist across sessions.

docs.title: Image Support
docs.text: You can paste images into the chat to provide visual references for the AI.

docs.title: Pasting Images
docs.text:
1. Copy an image to your clipboard (screenshot, design mockup, etc.)
  2. Click on the chat input field
  3. Press Ctrl+V (or Cmd+V on Mac)
  4. Image thumbnail appears in the preview area above the input
  5. Add your text description and send

docs.title: Use Cases for Images
docs.text:
• Design mockups: Paste a design and ask the AI to recreate it
  • Existing content: Show the AI what your current page looks like
  • Inspiration: Paste examples from other websites for reference
  • Bug reports: Show the AI what looks wrong so it can fix it

You can attach multiple images to a single message. Click the X on a thumbnail to remove it before sending.

docs.title: Page Screenshots
docs.text:
The AI can capture screenshots of your current page to better understand the existing design and match styles. When the AI needs to see your page, it uses the screenshot tool automatically.

This helps the AI match your store's fonts, colors, spacing, and overall design style.

docs.title: Storage Subscription (Optional)
docs.text:
The storage subscription is an optional add-on. It is not required to use the AI chatbot — you can generate code and use all AI features without it.

The subscription covers server-side storage for:

  • Chat history — your conversation messages are saved and restored across sessions
  • Uploaded images — images you paste into the chat are stored permanently on the server
  • Version history — all generated code versions are preserved and accessible anytime

docs.title: Without Subscription (Free)
docs.text:
Without a subscription, the widget still works fully, but storage is limited:

  • Chat history is stored in browser session storage only
  • History is lost when you close the browser tab
  • Limited to approximately 4 MB of data
  • Images are stored as temporary data in the browser
  • Version history is lost when the session ends

Note

The AI chatbot itself, code generation, model selection, and all AI features work the same with or without a subscription. The subscription only affects data persistence between sessions.

docs.title: With Active Subscription
docs.text:
• Chat messages, images, and code versions are saved to the server
  • Data persists across browser sessions and devices
  • Re-opening the chatbot restores your full conversation and all versions
  • Images are stored on the server for the duration of your subscription

docs.title: Free Trial
docs.text:
• A free trial starts automatically on first use
  • Trial lasts for 1 month
  • Full access to all storage features during the trial
  • No credit card required to start

docs.title: Trial Notifications
docs.text:
When the trial is active, you will see a notification:

"Chat history saving is on a free trial until [date]. After trial, chat data and code versions will be lost."

Available actions:

Action Description Auto-renew Enable automatic renewal to continue after trial ends Disable Turn off server storage (switch to browser-only) Dismiss Hide the notification

docs.title: After Trial
docs.text:
If auto-renew is enabled and you have sufficient credits:

  • Subscription renews automatically
  • Credits are deducted from your account balance
  • No interruption in service

If auto-renew is off or insufficient credits:

  • Subscription expires
  • Chat history, images, and versions fall back to browser storage
  • Existing server data is retained for 30 days before being deleted
  • You can reactivate at any time from the app Settings

docs.title: Managing Your Subscription
docs.text:
Subscription settings are available in the PapaThemes app in your BigCommerce admin under Settings.

From there you can:

  • View subscription status and expiration date
  • Enable or disable auto-renewal
  • Check your credit balance
  • Purchase additional credits

docs.title: How Much Does AI Content Generation Cost?
docs.text: AI usage is paid for using credits in your PapaThemes account. Credits are measured in USD cents — for example, a $10 top-up gives you 1,000 credits (cents).

docs.title: What Are Tokens?
docs.text:
Tokens are the unit the AI uses to measure text and images. Think of them as small chunks of content:

  • Text: Approximately 1 token per 3–4 characters of English text. For example, the sentence "Create a hero banner with a blue background" is roughly 12 tokens.
  • Images: Token count depends on image resolution. A typical pasted screenshot may use 1,000–5,000 tokens depending on size and detail. Higher resolution images use more tokens.
  • Code: Generated HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code also counts as tokens in the AI's response.

Both the text you send (input tokens) and the code the AI generates (output tokens) are counted together.

docs.title: Cost Formula
docs.text:
The cost for each AI response is calculated as:

Cost = (input tokens + output tokens) / 1,000,000 × price per model

Model Price per 1M tokens Mini $24 Standard $66 Max $108

Example: You send a message (2,000 input tokens) and the AI responds with code (4,000 output tokens) using the Standard model:

(2,000 + 4,000) / 1,000,000 × \(66 = **\)0.40** (0.40 cents deducted)

docs.title: What Counts Toward Tokens
docs.text:
Each AI request includes:

Component Description Token Impact Your message The text you type in the chat Based on text length Pasted images Images you attach to a message Based on image resolution Conversation history Previous messages in the chat Grows with each exchange Current code The existing widget code the AI references Based on code length Page screenshots When the AI captures your page Based on image resolution AI response Generated code and explanation Based on response length AI thinking The AI's internal reasoning process Based on complexity

Tip

Start a new conversation (Clear button) when working on a different design. This resets the conversation history and reduces token usage per request.

docs.title: How Credits Are Deducted
docs.text:
• Credits are deducted once per AI response, after the response is complete
  • The cost is rounded up to the nearest cent
  • If your credit balance is insufficient, the AI will show an error: "Insufficient credits. Please top up your account."
  • Your balance must be greater than zero to send a message

docs.title: Monitoring Your Usage
docs.text:
The token bar in the chatbot shows real-time usage for the current session:

  • Input / Output tokens: How many tokens have been sent and received
  • Total cost: Running cost in USD for this session
  • Context %: How much of the AI's memory is being used

docs.title: Checking Your Balance
docs.text:
• Your credit balance is visible in the PapaThemes app in BigCommerce admin
  • After each AI response, the chatbot receives your updated balance

docs.title: Purchasing Credits
docs.text: Credits can be purchased via PayPal in the PapaThemes app. Navigate to the Credits or Payment section in the app.

docs.title: Tips to Save Credits
docs.text:
• Use Mini model for small changes (text edits, color tweaks) — it costs 4.5x less than Max
  • Clear chat and start fresh when switching to a different design task
  • Be specific in your prompts to avoid back-and-forth refinements
  • Avoid pasting unnecessary large images — crop to the relevant area first

docs.title: Page Builder Settings
docs.text:
When you select the AI HTML widget in Page Builder, the settings panel shows:

Option Description Default HTML Content Code editor with the current HTML content Welcome card Show in Container Wraps the content in a centered container div false

Note

The HTML Content field shows the raw code. You typically don't need to edit this directly — use the AI chatbot instead. The content updates automatically when the AI generates new code.

Tip

Enable Show in Container if you want the widget content to be centered and constrained to the page's standard content width.

docs.title: Landing Page Hero Section
docs.text: "Create a hero section with a large background image, centered heading, subtitle text, and a call-to-action button. Use a dark overlay on the image for readability."

docs.title: Product Feature Grid
docs.text: "Build a 3-column grid showcasing product features. Each column should have an icon, a title, and a description. Make it responsive — stack to 1 column on mobile."

docs.title: Countdown Timer
docs.text: "Create a countdown timer to December 25th with days, hours, minutes, and seconds. Use a dark background with large white numbers."

docs.title: Customer Testimonials
docs.text: "Design a testimonials carousel with customer quotes, names, and star ratings. Show 3 testimonials on desktop, 1 on mobile. Auto-rotate every 5 seconds."

docs.title: FAQ Section
docs.text: "Build an FAQ accordion with 5 questions and answers. Clicking a question should expand/collapse the answer with smooth animation."

docs.title: Newsletter Signup
docs.text: "Create an email newsletter signup form with a heading, description, email input, and submit button. Style it with a gradient background."

docs.title: Writing Good Prompts
docs.text:
1. Be specific: Instead of "make a banner", say "create a full-width banner with a background image, white text centered vertically, and a blue CTA button"
  2. Mention responsive behavior: Tell the AI how the design should adapt on mobile (e.g., "stack columns on mobile")
  3. Reference your store's style: Say "match the blue color scheme of my store" — the AI can take screenshots to see your current design
  4. Iterate step by step: Start with the basic layout, then refine colors, spacing, and details in follow-up messages
  5. Use images: Paste a screenshot of a design you like and say "recreate this"

docs.title: Managing Token Usage
docs.text:
• Start new conversations for unrelated designs (use the Clear button)
  • Use Mini model for small text or color changes
  • Use Standard model for most design work
  • Use Max model only for complex, multi-component layouts
  • Watch the context percentage — if it gets high, start a fresh chat

docs.title: Common Follow-Up Requests
docs.text:
• "Make the heading larger"
  • "Change the button color to match my store's primary color"
  • "Add more spacing between sections"
  • "Make the text responsive for mobile"
  • "Add a hover effect to the cards"
  • "Change the layout to 2 columns instead of 3"

docs.title: Auto-Scoping
docs.text:
All generated code is automatically scoped to prevent conflicts with your store's existing styles. This means:

  • CSS classes are prefixed with a unique identifier
  • CSS variables are namespaced to the widget
  • JavaScript is wrapped in an isolated scope
  • Your store's theme styles are not affected by widget code

You don't need to do anything — this happens automatically.

docs.title: Chatbot Not Appearing
docs.text:
• Make sure you're in Page Builder edit mode
  • Check that the AI HTML widget is on the page
  • Try refreshing the Page Builder

docs.title: "Insufficient Credits" Error
docs.text:
• Your credit balance is empty
  • Purchase more credits in the PapaThemes app
  • Use the Mini model for lower cost

docs.title: Chat History Lost
docs.text:
• If your subscription expired, history falls back to browser storage
  • Browser storage is cleared when you close the tab
  • Reactivate your subscription to restore server-saved history (within 30-day retention)

docs.title: Code Not Applying
docs.text:
• The AI may still be generating — wait for the streaming to complete
  • Check the version control to see if the code was saved
  • Try clicking a previous version then coming back to the latest

docs.title: Context Too Large
docs.text:
• If the context percentage reaches 100%, the AI may lose track of earlier messages
  • Start a new conversation with the Clear button
  • The AI automatically manages context by removing old thinking blocks

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Page Builder only: The AI chatbot is only available when editing in Page Builder. On the live storefront, only the generated HTML content is displayed
  2. Credits required: Each AI interaction costs credits based on the model and token usage
  3. Model selection: Start with Standard for best balance of quality and cost. Switch to Mini for small tweaks or Max for complex designs
  4. Version history: Use version navigation to compare or revert changes — no work is lost
  5. Image pasting: Paste design mockups or screenshots to help the AI understand what you want
  6. Browser compatibility: The chatbot requires a modern browser with Shadow DOM support (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari)
  7. Subscription: Enable auto-renew before your trial ends to avoid losing server-stored chat history
  8. Data retention: After subscription expires, server data is kept for 30 days before being deleted

docs.title: What is the AI HTML Widget?
docs.text: The AI HTML Widget is a BigCommerce Page Builder component that lets you create custom HTML, CSS, and JavaScript content using AI. Describe what you want in plain language, and the AI generates production-ready code.

docs.title: Do I need coding experience?
docs.text: No coding experience is required. The widget uses a chat interface where you describe your desired content in plain English. The AI handles all the code generation.

docs.title: How much does it cost?
docs.text: The widget itself is free to use. AI usage is paid via credits. Costs range from $24 to $108 per million tokens depending on the AI model selected (Mini, Standard, or Max).

docs.title: Can I use it with any BigCommerce theme?
docs.text: Yes, the AI HTML Widget works with all BigCommerce themes, including Cornerstone and custom themes. Generated code is automatically scoped to prevent conflicts with your theme's styles.

docs.title: Is my chat history saved?
docs.text: By default, chat history is stored in your browser session only. With the optional Storage Subscription, conversations persist across sessions and devices.

docs.title: What can I create with this widget?
docs.text: You can create hero banners, testimonial sections, FAQ accordions, countdown timers, newsletter signup forms, product feature grids, image galleries, and any custom landing page sections you can describe.

docs.title: How do I access the pre-made blocks gallery?
docs.text: Open the AI chatbot sidebar by clicking the purple AI button in Page Builder. Below the chat input, click the "Browse Blocks" button to open the full-screen gallery with 300+ templates.

docs.title: What's the difference between Mini, Standard, and Max AI models?
docs.text: Mini (\(24/1M tokens) is fastest and best for simple edits. Standard (\)66/1M tokens) offers balanced quality for most tasks. Max ($108/1M tokens) provides highest quality for complex designs.

docs.title: Can I undo changes made by the AI?
docs.text: Yes. Every AI-generated change creates a new version. Use the version navigation arrows in the chatbot header to browse and restore any previous version.

docs.title: Does the generated content work on mobile devices?
docs.text: Yes. The AI chatbot is designed for desktop use within Page Builder, but all generated content is responsive and displays properly on mobile devices, tablets, and desktops

docs.title: Callout Icons Widget for BigCommerce Page Builder
Description
docs.text:
The Callout Icons Widget displays icons with descriptive text, commonly used to highlight features, services, or USPs (Unique Selling Points).

Page Builder Name: Callout Icons | PapaThemes Beautify

Version: 1.0.0

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ✅ SVG icons with sprite
  • ✅ Link support
  • ✅ Inline editing
  • ✅ Customizable colors
  • ✅ Responsive layout

docs.title: 1. Add Widget
docs.text:
1. Open Page Builder
  2. Drag Callout Icons | PapaThemes Beautify widget to the page
  3. Widget displays with default icons

docs.title: Icon Settings
docs.text: Option Description Icon Select icon from sprite Title Callout title Description Detailed description Link URL on click

docs.title: SVG Sprite
docs.text:
Widget uses SVG sprite injection:

SVGSpriteInject(iconUrl, {
    onInjected: (el) => {
        el.setAttribute('style', 'display:none');
    }
});

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Load once, use multiple times
  • Optimized performance
  • Can style with CSS

docs.title: Link Behavior
docs.text:
When a link is configured:

// Click handler
el.addEventListener('click', () => {
    if (!editMode) {
        window.location = el.dataset.link;
    }
});
  • Does not navigate in Edit Mode
  • Navigates normally in preview/live

docs.title: USP Bar
docs.text:
Icon 1: Free Shipping - Free shipping on orders over $50
Icon 2: 24/7 Support - Support available 24/7
Icon 3: Secure Payment - Safe and secure checkout
Icon 4: Easy Returns - 30-day return policy

docs.title: Service Features
docs.text:
Icon 1: Fast Delivery - 2-hour delivery available
Icon 2: Quality Products - Premium quality guaranteed
Icon 3: Best Price - Price match guarantee

docs.title: CSS Classes
docs.text:
.callout-icons {
    display: flex;
    justify-content: center;
}

.callout-icon-item {
    text-align: center;
    padding: 20px;
}

.callout-icon-item svg {
    width: 48px;
    height: 48px;
}

docs.title: Inline Editing
docs.text:
1. Enable Edit Mode
  2. Click on callout item
  3. Edit:
  4. Title
  5. Description
  6. Link
  7. Save changes

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Icons: Use icons from available sprite

The Chiara Flex Banners Widget is a special version of Flex Banners for the Chiara theme, featuring GSAP animation for parallax hover effects.

Version: 1.0.0

docs.title: Features

docs.title: 2. Configure GSAP Effect
docs.text: Each banner item has option: - Use GSAP: Enable/disable GSAP animation

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Widget uses GSAP for parallax effect:

  1. Second image (overlay) follows mouse movement
  2. Movement calculated based on relative position
  3. Scale and position animation

docs.title: Code Implementation
docs.text:
$widget.find('._banner')
    .mousemove(e => {
        const $el = $(e.currentTarget);
        const $target = $el.find('img + img'); // Second image

        if ($target.length > 0) {
            const relX = e.pageX - $el.offset().left;
            const relY = e.pageY - $el.offset().top;
            const movement = -50;

            gsap.to($target.get(0), 1, {
                x: (relX - $el.width() / 2) / $el.width() * movement,
                y: (relY - $el.height() / 2) / $el.height() * movement,
                scale: 1,
            });
        }
    })
    .mouseout(e => {
        gsap.to($target.get(0), 1, { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1 });
    });

docs.title: Cleanup
docs.text:
Widget automatically cleans up when removed:

const mo = new MutationObserver(mutations => {
    mutations.forEach(mutation => {
        if (mutation.removedNodes.length) {
            mutation.removedNodes.forEach(el => {
                if ($el.data('ckEditorInstance')) {
                    destroyCKEditor(el);
                }
            });
        }
    });
});

mo.observe(regionElement, { childList: true, subtree: true });

docs.title: Destroy CKEditor
docs.text:
const destroyCKEditor = (el) => {
    const editor = $el.data('ckEditorInstance');
    const content = editor?.getData();

    editor?.destroy().then(() => $el.html(content));
    $el.data('ckEditorInstance', null);
};

docs.title: Videos in Slides
docs.text:
Widget automatically manages video playback:

// Pause all videos on slide change
slider.onSlide(() => {
    slider.slides.forEach(slide => {
        slide.querySelectorAll('video').forEach(video => video.pause());
    });

    // Play current slide's video
    slider.slides[0].querySelectorAll('video').forEach(video => {
        if ($(video).is(':visible')) {
            video.play();
        }
    });
});

docs.title: Intersection Observer
docs.text:
Videos auto-pause when out of viewport:

const observer = new IntersectionObserver(entries => {
    entries.forEach(entry => {
        if (!entry.isIntersecting) {
            entry.target.pause();
        } else {
            // Play if current slide
            video.play();
        }
    });
});

docs.title: Random Banner Order
docs.text:
When Random Banner Order is enabled:

function shuffleSlides(widgetElement) {
    const trackContainer = widgetElement.querySelector('.blaze-track');
    const slides = Array.from(trackContainer.children);

    // Fisher-Yates shuffle
    for (let i = slides.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
        const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));
        [slides[i], slides[j]] = [slides[j], slides[i]];
    }

    slides.forEach(slide => trackContainer.appendChild(slide));
}
  • Shuffles on each page load
  • Content unchanged, only order

docs.title: Relative Link Fix
docs.text:
Widget automatically fixes relative links:

function fixRelativeLinks(widgetElement) {
    const elements = widgetElement.querySelectorAll('[data-editable-content] a');
    const regex = /^(?!\/|\w+:\/\/).*/;

    elements.forEach((el) => {
        const href = el.getAttribute('href');

        if (regex.test(href)) {
            el.setAttribute('href', `/${href}`);
        }
    });
}

docs.text:
function onMessage(event) {
    let data = JSON.parse(event.data);

    if (data.action === 'toggle-edit-mode' &&
        data.initiator === 'PAGE_BUILDER') {
        if (data.editMode) {
            show();
        } else {
            hide();
        }
    }
}

window.addEventListener('message', onMessage);

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
window.PapathemesWidgetResponsiveInit = () => {
    if (window.BigCommerce && window.BigCommerce.inlineEditor) {
        show(); // In Page Builder
    } else {
        hide(); // Preview/Live
    }

    // Prevent duplicate initialization
    if (window.PapathemesWidgetResponsive__onMessageInitialized) {
        return;
    }

    window.PapathemesWidgetResponsive__onMessageInitialized = true;
    window.addEventListener('message', onMessage);
};

• Hide large videos/images (still download)
  • Duplicate critical content
  • Forget to test mobile
  • Over-complicate layouts

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Edit mode: Content always visible when editing
  2. Download: Hidden content still downloads
  3. SEO: Hidden content still indexed
  4. Testing: Test on real devices
  5. CSS preference: Prefer CSS media queries when possible
  6. Duplicate init: Widget automatically prevents duplicate

docs.title: Section Templates Gallery
docs.text:
The AI HTML Widget includes a growing library of pre-made section templates you can apply to your BigCommerce storefront with a single click. After applying a template, use the AI chatbot to customize colors, text, images, and layout to match your brand.

This page showcases the Form Templates and Popup Templates collections. For the full library of 280+ section templates (hero banners, product showcases, testimonials, and more), open the Browse Blocks button inside the AI chatbot.

docs.title: How to Use Templates
docs.text:
1. Add the AI HTML Widget to your page in Page Builder
  2. Open the AI chatbot sidebar (purple AI button)
  3. Click Browse Blocks below the chat input
  4. Filter by category — select Form Templates or Popup Templates
  5. Preview any template with desktop, tablet, and mobile frames
  6. Click Use This Block to apply it to your widget
  7. Tell the AI what to customize: "Change the colors to match my brand"

For detailed instructions, see Pre-made Blocks Gallery.

docs.title: Form Templates
docs.text:
22 professionally designed form templates for collecting customer information, processing requests, and driving engagement. Each form is fully responsive, accessible, and styled with modern design patterns.

All form templates use the BigCommerce GraphQL submitContactUs API — submissions are delivered to your store's contact email automatically. No third-party integrations required.

General Contact Form

Classic contact form with name, email, phone, subject dropdown, and message fields. Clean layout with field validation and success confirmation.

Customer Feedback Form

Customer satisfaction survey with star rating, experience categories, and open-ended suggestion fields. Ideal for post-purchase follow-up.

Job Application Form

Careers page form with position selection, experience level, availability, cover letter, and portfolio links.

Event Registration Form

Event RSVP and registration with attendee details, ticket type, dietary preferences, and session selection.

Newsletter Signup Enhanced

Advanced newsletter form with interest categories, frequency preferences, and incentive offer. Goes beyond a basic email field.

SaaS Demo Request Form

B2B demo request form with company details, team size, use case description, and preferred demo time.

Consultation Booking Form

Service booking form with consultation type, preferred date/time, budget range, and project description.

Partnership Inquiry Form

B2B partnership and affiliate inquiry with company profile, partnership type, and proposal details.

Vendor Registration Form

Supplier onboarding form with business details, product categories, tax ID, and certification uploads.

Product Inquiry Form

Custom order and product inquiry with SKU reference, customization options, quantity, and budget range.

Return & Exchange Form

Post-purchase return/exchange request with order number, reason selection, and preferred resolution.

Warranty Claim Form

Warranty claim submission with product details, purchase date, issue description, and proof of purchase.

Gift Registry Form

Gift registry creation for weddings, baby showers, and events with date, preferences, and item categories.

Real Estate Inquiry Form

Property inquiry with budget range, location preferences, property type, and move-in timeline.

Healthcare Appointment Form

Medical appointment request with service type, insurance info, preferred provider, and medical notes.

Restaurant Reservation Form

Dining reservation with party size, date/time, seating preference, dietary restrictions, and special occasions.

Course Enrollment Form

Education enrollment with student info, course selection, experience level, and learning goals.

Auto Service Booking Form

Automotive service scheduling with vehicle details, service type, preferred date, and issue description.

Travel Trip Inquiry Form

Travel inquiry with destination, travel dates, number of travelers, budget, and trip preferences.

Fitness Membership Form

Gym membership inquiry with fitness goals, experience level, preferred schedule, and membership tier.

Home Services Estimate Form

Free estimate request for home services with property type, service needed, urgency, and budget range.

Pet Care Appointment Form

Pet care booking with pet details, service type (vet, grooming, boarding), vaccination status, and special needs.

docs.title: Popup Templates
docs.text: 22 conversion-focused popup templates for promotions, lead capture, compliance, and customer engagement. Each popup includes built-in triggers (timer delay, exit intent, or click), dismiss/remember logic with localStorage, and responsive design across all devices.

docs.title: Marketing & Promotions
docs.text:
Drive sales with urgency-based promotional popups.

Welcome Discount Popup

First-time visitor welcome with discount code and one-click copy-to-clipboard. Timer-triggered modal with backdrop overlay.

Flash Sale Countdown Popup

Live countdown timer creating urgency for limited-time offers. Dynamic days/hours/minutes/seconds display.

Free Shipping Bar

Floating top bar announcing free shipping threshold. Non-intrusive notification that stays visible during browsing.

Seasonal Promo Popup

Fullscreen takeover for major seasonal campaigns — Black Friday, Christmas, summer sales. High-impact visual design.

Exclusive Member Deal

VIP member-only offer with premium gold-accent design. Creates exclusivity and rewards loyal customers.

Limited Stock Urgency

Slide-in notification showing low stock count. FOMO-driven design to accelerate purchase decisions.

docs.title: Lead Capture & Newsletter
docs.text:
Grow your email list and capture leads with engaging signup forms.

Newsletter Signup Popup

Email subscription popup with incentive offer. Integrates with BigCommerce Storefront Subscribers API.

Exit Intent Offer

Triggered when visitors move to leave the page. Last-chance discount to retain potential customers.

Spin to Win

Gamified wheel popup where visitors spin to reveal discount codes. Fun, interactive lead capture with high engagement.

VIP Club Signup

Exclusive club registration with benefits list. Premium design that makes visitors feel valued for joining.

Content Download

Lead magnet popup for ebooks, guides, or catalogs. Slide-in format with email capture before download.

docs.title: Compliance & Information
docs.text:
Meet legal requirements and inform customers with professional notice popups.

Cookie Consent Banner

GDPR-compliant cookie notice with accept/decline options. Bottom bar format with privacy policy link.

Age Verification Gate

Full-screen age gate for restricted products (alcohol, tobacco). Blocks page access until verified.

Announcement Banner

Top bar for store-wide announcements — maintenance notices, policy updates, shipping delays, or news.

Store Locator

Location selector modal with store addresses, hours, and directions. Helps customers find nearby stores.

docs.title: Social & Engagement
docs.text:
Build community and drive engagement through social interaction popups.

Social Follow Prompt

Slide-in popup with social media icons encouraging followers. Supports Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, TikTok.

Customer Survey

Satisfaction survey with rating scale and feedback form. Collects actionable insights from customers.

Referral Program

Invite-a-friend popup with shareable link and email sharing. Reward-based referral system to grow your audience.

App Download Prompt

Mobile app promotion slide-in with App Store and Google Play badges. Targets mobile visitors.

docs.title: eCommerce-Specific
docs.text:
Purpose-built popups for common eCommerce scenarios.

Cart Reminder

Exit-intent cart abandonment popup reminding visitors of items in their cart. Reduces abandoned checkouts.

Back in Stock Alert

Email notification signup for out-of-stock products. Captures demand and notifies customers when items return.

Size Guide

Product size chart reference popup with measurement tables. Reduces returns by helping customers choose the right size.

docs.title: Customizing Templates
docs.text:
After applying any template, use the AI chatbot to customize it. Here are some common requests:

  • "Change the background color to #1a1a2e"
  • "Replace the placeholder text with our store's actual shipping policy"
  • "Update the discount code to SUMMER25"
  • "Change the popup trigger to show after 10 seconds instead of 5"
  • "Add our logo to the popup header"
  • "Make the form submit to a different email address"

Tip

Templates are a starting point. The AI can modify any aspect — colors, fonts, layout, text, images, timing, behavior, and more. Describe what you want and the AI will update the code.

docs.title: Popup Trigger Types
docs.text:
Popup templates support three trigger types. You can ask the AI to change the trigger after applying a template.

Trigger How It Works Best For Timer Delay Popup appears after a set number of seconds (default: 3-5s) Welcome offers, announcements Exit Intent Popup appears when the visitor's mouse moves toward the browser's close/back button Last-chance offers, cart reminders Click Popup appears when a specific button or link is clicked Size guides, store locators

All popups include dismiss memory — once a visitor closes the popup, it won't appear again during their session (stored in localStorage).

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: One-time free credit gift for all users (old + new). Extensible for future promotions. Controlled via environment variables. Grant happens on /load (when user opens admin app).

docs.title: User model — new field
docs.text:
creditPromotions: [{
    code: String,            // unique promo identifier, e.g. 'welcome_5usd'
    amountCents: Number,     // amount granted, e.g. 500
    claimedAt: Date,         // when credit was granted
    noticeDismissedAt: Date, // null = chatbot notice not yet dismissed
}]

docs.title: ApiUsageLog — new enum value
docs.text: Add 'credit_promo' to subscriptionEvent enum. Log entry includes: - storeHash, subscriptionEvent: 'credit_promo' - amountCents, balanceBeforeCents, balanceAfterCents - Promo code stored via existing fields or log context

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
CREDIT_PROMO_CODE=welcome_5usd    # empty string or unset = feature disabled
CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS=500            # amount in cents ($5 = 500)

docs.title: /load endpoint (server/routers/load.js)
docs.text:
After verifyPayload + verifyUser, before building response:

  1. Read CREDIT_PROMO_CODE and CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS from env
  2. If code is empty or cents <= 0 → skip (feature disabled)
  3. Check if user.creditPromotions already contains entry with matching code
  4. If not claimed → atomic findOneAndUpdate: 
    User.findOneAndUpdate(
    {
        _id: user._id,
        'creditPromotions.code': { $ne: PROMO_CODE },
    },
    {
        $push: {
            creditPromotions: {
                code: PROMO_CODE,
                amountCents: PROMO_CENTS,
                claimedAt: new Date(),
                noticeDismissedAt: null,
            }
        },
        $inc: { creditBalance: PROMO_CENTS },
    },
    { new: true }
);
  5. If update succeeded → add creditPromoAwarded: { code, amountCents } to response
  6. Fire-and-forget log to ApiUsageLog with subscriptionEvent: 'credit_promo'

docs.title: Chatbot notice dismiss endpoint
docs.text:
New route or extend existing /ai routes:

POST /ai/dismiss-promo-notice
Body: { store_hash, code }

Sets noticeDismissedAt on the matching creditPromotions entry: 

User.findOneAndUpdate(
    { context: `stores/${storeHash}`, 'creditPromotions.code': code },
    { $set: { 'creditPromotions.$.noticeDismissedAt': new Date() } }
);

docs.title: Chatbot promo status
docs.text:
Extend existing chatbot data flow (e.g. subscription status check) to also return: 

{
    "activePromo": {
        "code": "welcome_5usd",
        "amountCents": 500,
        "noticeDismissed": false
    }
}

Only returned when a matching promo exists and noticeDismissedAt === null.

docs.title: Redux state
docs.text: fetchAuth response now may include creditPromoAwarded. Store in auth slice.

docs.title: Gift Banner
docs.text: When creditPromoAwarded is present in load response: - Show a top banner (styled differently from PromoBanner — gift/celebration theme) - Content: "You've received $X.XX free credits! Try our AI HTML Generator." - CTA "Use Now" button → setActiveTab('widgets') + setHighlightWidgetName('AI HTML Generator | PapaThemes') - Dismiss (X) button hides banner (session only, no persistence needed — it only shows on first load when credit is granted)

docs.title: Inline notice in chatbox
docs.text: When chatbot sidebar opens and state.messages.length === 0: 1. Fetch promo status from server (piggyback on existing subscription check or separate call) 2. If activePromo exists and !noticeDismissed: - Show inline notice via addInlineNotice(): "You have $X free credits! Start chatting to create HTML sections with AI." 3. When user sends first message: - Call POST /ai/dismiss-promo-notice (fire-and-forget) - Remove the inline notice element from DOM

docs.title: No localStorage
docs.text: All state is DB-backed. Once noticeDismissedAt is set, no chatbox instance will show the notice again.

docs.title: Flow Diagram
docs.text:
User opens admin app
  → POST /load
  → Server checks CREDIT_PROMO_CODE env
  → User.creditPromotions has code?
    ├─ No  → atomic grant + log → response includes creditPromoAwarded
    │        → Client shows gift banner with "Use Now" CTA
    └─ Yes → normal response

User opens chatbot sidebar
  → messages.length === 0?
    ├─ Yes → fetch promo status
    │        → activePromo && !noticeDismissed?
    │          ├─ Yes → show inline notice
    │          └─ No  → nothing
    └─ No  → nothing

User sends first message in chatbot
  → dismiss promo notice (fire-and-forget API call)
  → remove notice from DOM
  → all future chatbox opens: noticeDismissedAt is set → no notice

docs.title: Future Extensibility
docs.text: To add a new promotion: 1. Change env vars: CREDIT_PROMO_CODE=summer_2026, CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS=300 2. All users who haven't claimed summer_2026 will get it on next app load 3. Previous promotions remain in creditPromotions array (audit trail) 4. Each promo has independent notice dismiss state

docs.title: Credit Promotion System — Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For Claude: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

Goal: Grant one-time free credit to all users on app load, with admin banner and chatbot inline notice.

Architecture: Extensible creditPromotions[] array on User model, controlled by env vars. Server grants on /load, logs to ApiUsageLog. Chatbot reads promo status via subscription endpoint piggyback. Notice dismiss persisted in DB.

Tech Stack: Node.js/Express, MongoDB/Mongoose, React/Redux (admin app), vanilla JS Shadow DOM (chatbot widget)

docs.title: Task 4: Server — credit promo logic module
docs.text:
Files: - Create: server/lib/credit-promo.js

Step 1: Create the module

const mongoose = require('mongoose');
const User = mongoose.model('User');
const ApiUsageLog = require('../models/ApiUsageLog');
const logger = require('./logger');

const PROMO_CODE = (process.env.CREDIT_PROMO_CODE || '').trim();
const PROMO_CENTS = parseInt(process.env.CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS || '0', 10);

/**
 * Attempt to grant the active credit promo to a user (idempotent).
 * Returns { awarded: true, code, amountCents } if granted, or { awarded: false }.
 */
async function tryGrantPromo(user) {
    if (!PROMO_CODE || PROMO_CENTS <= 0) return { awarded: false };

    // Already claimed this promo?
    if (user.creditPromotions?.some(p => p.code === PROMO_CODE)) {
        return { awarded: false };
    }

    const balanceBefore = user.creditBalance || 0;

    const updated = await User.findOneAndUpdate(
        {
            _id: user._id,
            'creditPromotions.code': { $ne: PROMO_CODE },
        },
        {
            $push: {
                creditPromotions: {
                    code: PROMO_CODE,
                    amountCents: PROMO_CENTS,
                    claimedAt: new Date(),
                    noticeDismissedAt: null,
                },
            },
            $inc: { creditBalance: PROMO_CENTS },
        },
        { new: true }
    );

    if (!updated) return { awarded: false };

    // Fire-and-forget log
    const storeHash = user.context?.replace('stores/', '') || '';
    ApiUsageLog.create({
        storeHash,
        endpoint: 'admin-credit-adjustment',
        subscriptionEvent: 'credit_promo',
        amountCents: PROMO_CENTS,
        balanceBeforeCents: balanceBefore,
        balanceAfterCents: updated.creditBalance,
    }).catch(err => logger.error('CreditPromo.log', err));

    return { awarded: true, code: PROMO_CODE, amountCents: PROMO_CENTS };
}

/**
 * Get the active promo status for chatbot notice.
 * Returns { code, amountCents, noticeDismissed } or null.
 */
function getActivePromo(user) {
    if (!PROMO_CODE || PROMO_CENTS <= 0) return null;

    const entry = user.creditPromotions?.find(p => p.code === PROMO_CODE);
    if (!entry) return null;

    return {
        code: entry.code,
        amountCents: entry.amountCents,
        noticeDismissed: entry.noticeDismissedAt != null,
    };
}

/**
 * Dismiss the promo notice for a user (set noticeDismissedAt).
 */
async function dismissPromoNotice(storeHash, code) {
    await User.findOneAndUpdate(
        { context: `stores/${storeHash}`, 'creditPromotions.code': code },
        { $set: { 'creditPromotions.$.noticeDismissedAt': new Date() } }
    );
}

module.exports = { tryGrantPromo, getActivePromo, dismissPromoNotice };

Step 2: Commit

git add server/lib/credit-promo.js
git commit -m "feat(server): add credit-promo module with grant, status, dismiss"

Step 2: Piggyback promo onto subscription status response

Find the subHandlers.status method. In its response, add activePromo field.

Locate the status handler (it returns subscription status JSON). After computing the response object, add:

            // Piggyback credit promo status
            const activePromo = getActivePromo(res.locals.user);

And include activePromo in the response JSON.

Step 3: Add dismiss endpoint

After the subscription route registration block (line ~1279), add:

router.post('/dismiss-promo-notice', verifyRequestToken, async (req, res) => {
    try {
        const { code } = req.body;
        if (!code) return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Promo code is required' });
        await dismissPromoNotice(res.locals.storeHash, code);
        res.json({ success: true });
    } catch (error) {
        logger.error('CreditPromo.dismiss', error, { req });
        res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to dismiss promo notice' });
    }
});

docs.title: Compact Conversation Feature - Requirements & Acceptance Criteria
1. Problem Statement
docs.text:
As conversations grow longer, every subsequent message re-sends the entire conversation history to the Claude API. On a 20-message conversation, the input tokens can reach 100K+, costing the merchant significant credits per message. Today's auto-compaction only triggers when the context window is nearly full (~180K tokens), meaning merchants pay for inflated input tokens long before that threshold.

Merchant pain point: "I'm 15 messages in, my context stats show 80K tokens used, and each new message costs me ~$0.24 in input alone. I want to keep my conversation but stop paying for the full history on every call."

docs.title: 2. Target Persona
docs.text:
• Primary: Power users who build complex widgets over 10-30+ message sessions
  • Secondary: Cost-conscious merchants on small credit balances who want to extend their budget
  • Technical skill: Low to medium (the UI must make it obvious what compaction does)

docs.title: 3. Value Proposition
docs.text:
Aspect Current State After Compact Input tokens per message (20-msg session) ~80,000-120,000 ~5,000-15,000 Cost per follow-up message (Sonnet) ~\(0.24-\)0.36 ~\(0.015-\)0.045 Token savings None (until auto-compact) 70-90% reduction on input tokens Conversation context quality Full verbatim history Summarized key decisions + current code + recent messages

Core differentiator from existing auto-compact: User-initiated, happens much earlier in the conversation, and the user retains full visibility of their chat history in the UI.

docs.title: 4. How It Differs from Existing Auto-Compaction
docs.text:
The server already has compactMessages() in ai-token-manager.js which auto-triggers when context exceeds ~180K tokens. The new feature differs:

Dimension Existing Auto-Compact New User-Initiated Compact Trigger Automatic when near context limit User clicks a button Timing Very late (80%+ context used) Any time after first AI response Persistence Transient (only for that API call) Persisted -- subsequent messages use compacted version UI feedback None (invisible to user) Inline notice + context stats update Full history Lost (messages truncated/summarized in state) Preserved in UI and in DB

docs.title: US-1: Compact Button Appearance
docs.text:
As a merchant using the AI chatbot, I want to see a "Compact Conversation" tip after each AI response completes, so that I know I can reduce my token costs at any point.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After each completed assistant message (not during streaming), a hint element appears below the message in the chat flow. 2. The hint displays: a compress/shrink icon + "Compact conversation to save tokens" (or similar copy). 3. The hint is styled as a subtle, non-intrusive tip (similar to the existing "Continue" button pattern -- light background, small text, icon + text + action button). 4. The hint does NOT appear after the very first assistant response (nothing to compact yet -- need at least 2 user+assistant exchanges). 5. The hint does NOT appear if the conversation is already compacted and no new messages have been added since. 6. The hint disappears when the user starts typing a new message or when they click compact. 7. If the conversation is already compacted and new messages are added, the hint reappears (re-compaction is possible).

docs.title: US-2: Compact Action Execution
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want clicking "Compact" to summarize my conversation history via the Claude API, so that future messages are cheaper without losing context quality.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. Clicking the compact button sends a request to a new server endpoint: POST /ai/compact-conversation. 2. The server uses claude-haiku-4-5 (cheapest model) to generate the summary, regardless of the user's selected model. 3. The summary prompt instructs Claude to preserve: - Key design decisions and requirements discussed - Current widget code (the latest <generated_code> block) - Any referenced image descriptions - User preferences expressed during the conversation - Error resolutions and fixes applied 4. The compacted result is a single [{ role: 'user', content: '<conversation_summary>...' }, { role: 'assistant', content: 'I understand...' }] message pair (matching existing hybridCompact format). 5. The compact API call costs credits (charged like any other AI call). The cost is shown to the user before confirming. 6. During compaction, a "Compacting conversation..." indicator appears (reuse thinking dots pattern). 7. If compaction fails (API error, insufficient credits), show an error notification and leave the conversation unchanged.

docs.title: US-3: Post-Compact Message Sending
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want messages sent after compacting to use ONLY the compacted summary (not full history) as context, so that I actually save tokens/credits.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After compaction, state.compactedMessages holds the summary + any new messages added after compaction. 2. When sending to /ai/chat-pb, the request body's messages field contains ONLY state.compactedMessages (not state.messages). 3. The full state.messages array remains unchanged and is still used for UI display. 4. The /context stats endpoint reflects the reduced token count post-compaction. 5. Token usage reported after compaction should be significantly lower than before (testable via /context command).

docs.title: US-4: Full History Preservation in UI
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want to still see my complete conversation history in the chat UI after compacting, so that I don't lose any information visually.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After compaction, all messages remain visible in the chat container -- no messages are removed or collapsed. 2. An inline notice appears in the chat flow at the compaction point: "Conversation compacted -- future messages will use a summarized context to save tokens." 3. Closing and reopening the chat widget shows the full message history (loaded from state.messages / DB). 4. The version history (code versions) is unaffected by compaction.

docs.title: US-5: Persistence of Compacted State
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want the compacted state to persist across chat sessions, so that reopening the chat doesn't lose the compaction benefit.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. The ChatHistory model stores a new field: compactedMessages (array, same structure as messages). 2. The ChatHistory model stores compactedAt (Date) indicating when last compaction occurred. 3. On saveChatHistory(), both messages (full) and compactedMessages (compacted) are saved. 4. On loadChatHistory(), if compactedMessages exists and is non-empty, it is loaded into state.compactedMessages. 5. For sessionStorage fallback (non-subscribers), compactedMessages is also saved/loaded.

docs.title: US-6: Re-Compaction and New Messages After Compact
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want to be able to compact again after adding more messages to a previously compacted conversation, so that I can keep costs low throughout a long session.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After compaction, new user/assistant messages are appended to BOTH state.messages (full history) and state.compactedMessages. 2. If the user clicks compact again, a NEW compaction runs on state.compactedMessages (not full history -- this is cheaper because compactedMessages is already small + new messages). 3. The compaction hint reappears after 2+ new message exchanges post-compact. 4. There is no limit on how many times the user can compact. 5. Each re-compaction replaces the previous compactedMessages with a new summary.

docs.title: US-7: Context Stats Reflect Compaction
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want the /context command to show whether my conversation is compacted and the token savings, so that I can verify the cost reduction.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. The context stats panel shows a "Compaction" section with: - Status: "Active" (green) or "Not compacted" - Original message count vs. API message count - Estimated token savings (original tokens - compacted tokens) - Last compacted timestamp 2. The utilization bar reflects the compacted token count (not the full history count).

docs.title: ChatHistory Model (MongoDB)
docs.text:
Add to server/models/ChatHistory.js:

// Compacted conversation for API calls (saves tokens)
compactedMessages: [{
    role: {
        type: String,
        enum: ['user', 'assistant'],
        required: true
    },
    content: {
        type: mongoose.Schema.Types.Mixed,
        required: true
    }
}],
compactedAt: {
    type: Date,
    default: null
}

docs.title: Client State
docs.text:
Add to client/widgets/chatbot/state.js:

// Compact conversation state
compactedMessages: null,    // Array of messages to send to API (null = use full messages)
compactedAt: null,          // Date of last compaction

docs.title: POST /ai/compact-conversation
docs.text:
Request: 

{
    "store_hash": "abc123",
    "request_token": "...",
    "instanceId": "widget-xyz",
    "messages": [...],
    "currentCode": "...",
    "model": "claude-haiku-4-5"
}

Processing: 1. Verify store_hash and request_token. 2. Check credit balance (estimate compaction cost: ~1K-2K output tokens on Haiku = ~\(0.001-\)0.002). 3. Build summary prompt: 

Summarize this AI widget-building conversation concisely.
Preserve: key design decisions, user requirements, current code state,
image descriptions referenced, errors encountered and resolved,
user preferences for style/layout. Be thorough but concise.
Current code: [latest generated_code block]
Conversation: [messages as user/assistant pairs, truncated to 500 chars each]
4. Call Claude Haiku with the summary prompt. 5. Return the compacted messages pair. 6. Charge credits for the API call.

Response (success): 

{
    "compactedMessages": [
        { "role": "user", "content": "<conversation_summary>...</conversation_summary>" },
        { "role": "assistant", "content": "I understand the context..." }
    ],
    "usage": {
        "input_tokens": 5000,
        "output_tokens": 1500
    },
    "cost_cents": 0.2
}

Response (error): 

{
    "error": "Insufficient credits for compaction"
}

docs.title: Files to Modify
docs.text: File Changes client/widgets/chatbot/state.js Add compactedMessages, compactedAt to state client/widgets/chatbot/send-message.js Use state.compactedMessages (if set) instead of state.messages in API request body client/widgets/chatbot/messages.js Render compact hint after assistant messages; render inline compaction notice client/widgets/chatbot/api.js Add compactConversation() API call; update saveChatHistory/loadChatHistory to include compacted state client/widgets/chatbot/context-stats.js Add compaction section to stats display client/widgets/chatbot/styles.js Add styles for compact hint, inline notice server/models/ChatHistory.js Add compactedMessages, compactedAt fields server/routers/ai.js Add POST /ai/compact-conversation endpoint; modify /ai/chat-pb to accept compacted state

docs.title: Key Change in send-message.js
docs.text:
In the sendMessage function, at line ~291-302 where the fetch request is built:

// BEFORE (current):
body: JSON.stringify({
    ...
    messages: state.messages,
    ...
})

// AFTER:
body: JSON.stringify({
    ...
    messages: state.compactedMessages || state.messages,
    ...
})

After receiving a response, append the new user+assistant pair to BOTH arrays:

// Existing: state.messages.push(...)
// New: also push to compactedMessages if it exists
if (state.compactedMessages) {
    state.compactedMessages.push(
        { role: 'user', content: messageContent },
        { role: 'assistant', content: finalText }
    );
}

docs.title: 9. Edge Cases
docs.text: Edge Case Handling User compacts with only 1 exchange Compact hint hidden; minimum 2 exchanges required Compaction API call fails Show error notification, state unchanged, hint remains Insufficient credits for compaction Show specific "insufficient credits" message with cost estimate User sends message during compaction Queue the message (existing queue system handles this) Conversation has images Summary preserves image descriptions but not base64 data; images remain in full history User does "New Chat" after compacting Both messages and compactedMessages are cleared Non-subscriber (sessionStorage) compactedMessages saved/loaded from sessionStorage alongside messages Very short conversation (< 5K tokens) Compact hint still appears but a warning: "Conversation is small -- compaction may not save significant tokens" Compacted state loaded but server changes compactedMessages treated as source of truth for API calls until next compact or new chat

docs.title: Scenario: 20-message widget-building session on Claude Sonnet
docs.text:
Without compaction: - Messages 1-10: Average ~50K input tokens per call = ~\(0.15/call - Messages 11-20: Average ~100K input tokens per call = ~\)0.30/call - Total input cost: ~$2.25

With compaction after message 10: - Messages 1-10: Same as above = ~\(0.15/call avg = ~\)1.50 - Compaction call (Haiku): ~5K input + 1.5K output = ~\(0.002 - Messages 11-20: Average ~15K input tokens per call = ~\)0.045/call - Total input cost: ~$1.50 + \(0.002 + ~\)0.45 = ~$1.95

Savings: ~$0.30 (13%) for one compaction, more with multiple compactions

For longer sessions (30+ messages) or conversations with images/code, savings compound to 30-50%.

docs.title: Phase 1 (MVP) -- Recommended Start
docs.text:
• Compact button as inline hint after assistant messages
  • Server endpoint using Haiku for summarization
  • Compacted state persisted in DB and sessionStorage
  • Basic inline notice at compaction point
  • Credit charge for compaction call

docs.title: Phase 2
docs.text:
• Context stats showing compaction savings
  • "Compact & Continue" option (compact + send queued message in one action)
  • Auto-suggest compaction when context reaches 50% utilization
  • Preview of summary before confirming compaction

docs.title: Phase 3
docs.text:
• Auto-compact preferences in admin settings (compact automatically at X% utilization)
  • Compaction quality feedback ("Was this summary accurate?")
  • Smart compaction that preserves more detail for code-heavy conversations

docs.title: 12. Success Metrics
docs.text: Metric Target Measurement Adoption rate 30% of 10+ message sessions use compact Track compact_conversation events in analytics Token savings 50%+ reduction in input tokens for subsequent messages Compare lastActualInputTokens before vs. after compaction Credit extension Merchants with compaction get 25%+ more messages per dollar Compare messages-per-credit for compacting vs. non-compacting sessions Conversation quality No increase in "the AI forgot context" complaints Monitor follow-up messages that reference earlier context Feature cost efficiency Compaction call costs < 5% of tokens saved Compare compaction API cost vs. cumulative input token savings

docs.title: 13. Technical Risks & Mitigations
docs.text: Risk Likelihood Impact Mitigation Summary loses critical context (e.g., specific CSS requirements) Medium High Include current code in summary prompt; keep last 4 messages verbatim alongside summary Compaction cost offsets savings for short conversations Low Low Only show hint after 2+ exchanges; show cost estimate before confirming Race condition: user sends message while compaction is in progress Medium Medium Set state.isCompacting flag; queue messages during compaction (reuse existing queue) Summary prompt itself is too large (very long conversations) Low Medium Truncate each message to 500 chars in summary prompt (matching existing summarizeMessages pattern) Compacted state diverges from full history after many messages Medium Low Re-compaction resolves this; show hint again after 5+ new exchanges

docs.title: 14. Open Questions for Product Decision
docs.text:
1. Should compaction be free for the first use per session? This could encourage adoption without merchants worrying about paying to save money.
  2. Should we show a preview of the summary before applying? Adds UX complexity but gives merchants confidence. Recommendation: Phase 2.
  3. Should the compact hint auto-dismiss after a timeout? Risk of being annoying vs. being discoverable. Recommendation: Dismiss on next user input, no auto-timeout.
  4. Should compaction preserve the exact latest code block verbatim (not summarized)? Recommendation: Yes -- always include the full current code in the compacted context.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
Three targeted bug fixes identified from the MongoDB ErrorLog digest:

  1. WelcomeEmail.day0 SMTP timeouts — Day 0 welcome emails failing ~17 times/month
  2. CreditPromo.paymentLog MongoDB noise — fire-and-forget timeout errors polluting error digest
  3. FetchError/retrieveChannelSites missing storeHash — can't identify which store triggers 401s

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: Lambda cold starts add 10–15s of network latency before an SMTP connection stabilises. Current connectionTimeout and greetingTimeout are 10s — too tight. Retry logic uses err.code matching, but some nodemailer errors ("Greeting never received", "Timeout") may not set err.code consistently, so retries are skipped.

docs.title: Change
docs.text:
• Increase connectionTimeout 10s → 20s
  • Increase greetingTimeout 10s → 20s
  • Increase socketTimeout 15s → 30s
  • Increase max attempts 2 → 3
  • Retry delays: 2s, 5s (exponential)
  • Expand retry condition: err.code match OR err.message matches /timeout|socket|connection/i

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text: • No change to Gmail credentials or transport; AWS SES not in scope.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: PaymentLog.create() and ApiUsageLog.create() are fire-and-forget. When a Lambda instance has a stale MongoDB TLS connection, these fail with MongoNetworkTimeoutError. The records are silently lost, which is a data integrity problem (PaymentLog is a financial audit trail).

docs.title: Change
docs.text:
• Introduce withRetry(fn, label) helper that catches transient MongoDB network errors (MongoNetworkError, MongoNetworkTimeoutError, ECONNRESET, ETIMEDOUT) and retries immediately (no delay, so the retry completes within the same Lambda invocation before the event loop is frozen).
  • On first transient failure: console.warn(...) so CloudWatch has a trace even if Lambda freezes before the retry runs.
  • On final failure (or non-transient error): logger.error(...) so the error reaches MongoDB ErrorLog and triggers the email digest.
  • Both ApiUsageLog.create() and PaymentLog.create() are wrapped with withRetry.

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text:
• No fix to the underlying MongoDB stale-connection root cause (Lambda TLS reuse).

Note: Writes are now awaited via Promise.allSettled to guarantee completion within the Lambda invocation (callbackWaitsForEmptyEventLoop=false would otherwise freeze unawaited promises). Response latency increases by the write time on the happy path (typically <100ms); this is acceptable given PaymentLog is a financial audit trail.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: logger.error('Utils.retrieveChannelSites', err) is called without req context, so extractRequestInfo cannot extract storeHash from res.locals.payload.store_hash. checkFetchError similarly logs FetchError without any request context.

docs.title: Change
docs.text:
• In retrieveChannelSites catch block: pass { req } to logger.error. Express sets req.res = res, so req.res.locals.payload.store_hash is accessible via the existing extractRequestInfo logic.
  • checkFetchError is called in many places without access to req; no change needed there — the retrieveChannelSites catch log will now carry storeHash context.

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text: • No special 401 handling or re-auth redirect (separate concern).

docs.title: Files Changed
docs.text: File Change server/lib/email.js Increase timeouts, 3 attempts, expanded retry condition server/lib/credit-promo.js Add withRetry(...) around fire-and-forget writes; console.warn(err.stack) on first transient failure; logger.error(...) on final/non-transient failure server/lib/utils.js Add { req } to retrieveChannelSites error log

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• email.js: existing unit tests for retry logic; add cases for err.message-based retry
  • credit-promo.js: verify immediate retry on transient MongoDB/network errors, console.warn on first transient failure, and logger.error on final/non-transient failure
  • utils.js: manual verify in next error digest that storeHash is populated for 401 errors
